CREATE TABLE docs (id INT, text TEXT);
INSERT INTO docs (id, text) VALUES (1, 'This is a test for the virtual database functionality.');
INSERT INTO docs (id, text) VALUES (2, 'Evaluating performance tuning and optimization techniques.');
INSERT INTO docs (id, text) VALUES (3, 'Graphics optimization for user interfaces.');
INSERT INTO docs (id, text) VALUES (4, 'Once upon a time in a land far away.');
SELECT count(*) FROM docs WHERE contains(text, 'virtual database');
SELECT id, SCORE FROM docs WHERE contains(text, 'performance and (tuning or optimization)');
SELECT id FROM docs WHERE contains(text, 'graphics and not (graphics near user near interface)');
SELECT id FROM docs WHERE contains(text, '"sql interfac*"');
SELECT id FROM docs WHERE contains(text, '"dragon*" and not "once upon a time"');
SELECT d.id FROM docs d JOIN docs d2 ON d.id <> d2.id WHERE contains(d.text, 'performance') AND contains(d2.text, 'optimization');
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT id FROM docs WHERE contains(text, 'virtual database')) AS subquery;
SELECT id, text, SCORE FROM docs WHERE SCORE > 0.5 AND contains(text, 'graphics') ORDER BY SCORE DESC;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM docs WHERE contains(text, 'test') AND id IN (SELECT id FROM docs WHERE contains(text, 'functionality'));
SELECT id FROM docs WHERE contains(text, '"once upon a time"') AND id IN (SELECT id FROM docs WHERE contains(text, 'land'));
SELECT d1.id FROM docs d1, docs d2 WHERE contains(d1.text, 'tuning') AND contains(d2.text, 'performance') AND d1.id != d2.id;
